name: pytolith

metadata.name: Pytolith
metadata.summary: A simple package for reading Halo tag files.

documentation: A simple python library for reading (and perhaps writing in the future) Halo 2 tag files. Allows for tags to be introspected on any platform without the hassle of using the official tools.

variables.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TAG_FIELD_COLOR_VALYES
documentation: float tuple colors + int for integer colors

variables.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TAG_FIELD_POINT_VALUES
documentation: point2d type for integer 2d points + float tuples for real 2d/3d points and vectors

variables.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TAG_FIELD_ARRAY_VALUE
documentation: tuple of array entries, which themselves are tuples of fields

methods.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TagLayoutConfig.add_missing_fields
documentation: Add in missing fields (but doesn't fill the data in)

classes.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TagFieldElement

methods.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TagFieldElement.get_by_name
documentation: Lookup a field by name, raises an index error if it doesn't exist

methods.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TagFieldElement.get_by_c_name
documentation: Lookup a field by c-style name (snake_case), raises an index error  if it doesn't exist

methods.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TagFieldElement.get_by_pascal_name
documentation: Lookup a field by PascalCase name, raises an index error if it doesn't exist
Wrapper around a tuple of tag fields, allows accessing fields by their snake_case or PascalCase names not just their index

methods.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TagGroupData.fields
documentation: Tuple containing all fields for this tag

methods.name: Pytolith.TagTypes.TagGroupData.layout
documentation: Versioned tag layout defintion for this tag

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Fields.FieldNameInfo.is_block_name_source
documentation: should the block element name be set using this field?

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Fields.TagOptions.unique_id
documentation: regolith ID

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Fields.FieldDef.block_index_definition_reference_id
documentation: tag block index fields only

methods.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Fields.FieldWithOptionsDef.entries
documentation: flags or enums for this field

methods.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Fields.FieldWithOptionsDef.unique_id
documentation: Unique regolith ID or None if enum is anonymous.

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Fields.FieldWithOptionsDef.options
documentation: Options object for this field, None if the enum is anonymous. You can access the entries directly using `entries` if you are just inspecting the field.

methods.name: Pytolith.TagLoader.Header.EngineTag.__gt__
documentation: from https://stackoverflow.com/a/58362774

methods.name: Pytolith.TagLoader.Header.Header.old_fieldset_header
documentation: Should the old (short) fieldset header be used?

methods.name: Pytolith.TagLoader.Header.Header.old_string_id_format
documentation: Should string ID be included in-line?

methods.name: Pytolith.TagLoader.Header.Header.include_useless_padding
documentation: Should useless padding be included in the tag?

class_variables.name: Pytolith.TagLoader.Header.Header.data_size
documentation: not used anymore

class_variables.name: Pytolith.TagLoader.Header.Header.current_foundation_index
documentation: -1 means not in foundation file

methods.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.merged_fields
documentation: Returns the fields after accounting for any parent tags

methods.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef._loader_set_merged_fields
documentation: Internal setting for merged fields, can only be set once

methods.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef._loader_merge_fields_set
documentation: INTERNAL: Used by the loader to check if any required field merging operations are complete

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.version
documentation: Field set version.

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.sizeof_value
documentation: `sizeof` the field set in the build the defintions are extracted from. Don't trust this value to be accurate, it is only provided as an aid when debugging.

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.parent_version
documentation: If layout uses inheritance which version of the parent tag layout should we merge with?

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.sizeof_override
documentation: Override the legacy field lenght (length used when there is no tag field header)

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.auto_c_name_to_field_index
documentation: Autogenerated c_name to field index mapping

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.auto_pascal_name_to_field_index
documentation: Autogenerated PascalName to field index mapping

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.__merged_fields
documentation: backing store for merged fields

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.FieldSetDef.sizeof_source
documentation: `sizeof` source string extracted from tag defintions. Using this for anything is highly not recommended

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.LayoutDef.unique_id
documentation: Unique ID indentifying an `block:` or `struct:`

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.LayoutDef.internal_name
documentation: Block/structure name extracted from the binary may be non-unique, `unique_id` if you need a unique name.

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.LayoutDef.display_name
documentation: Human readable name for the tag block if one is set

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.LayoutDef.is_structure
documentation: Is this a structure or is this a tag block?

class_variables.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Layout.LayoutDef.structure_tag
documentation:
Tag used for the tag field data header. Ignored for non-structure layouts.

(default value for tag blocks is 'tbfd', which might stand for "tag block field data")

methods.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Package.Definitions.is_loaded
documentation: Are the tag defintions loaded?

methods.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Package.Definitions.dumps
documentation: Pickle and compress the definitions data

methods.name: Pytolith.Definitions.Package.Definitions.loads
documentation: Loads definitions dumped using `definitions.dump()`
